title: Tesla Austin Unsupervised Robotaxi Fleet Reaches 10 Model Ys
summary: Tesla's Austin Robotaxi fleet achieved a record of 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles operating driverless.
impact_reason: Mildly positive milestone confirming incremental growth in Austin's unsupervised fleet size, supporting steady scaling momentum in the primary hub.

title: Tesla's Dallas and Houston Robotaxi Fleets Reach Four Vehicles Total
summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data shows Tesla deployed one unsupervised vehicle each in Dallas and Houston at launch on April 18, increasing to two per city by April 20. Austin currently has 13 unsupervised vehicles active in the last 30 days.
impact_reason: Small initial deployments in new cities mark the start of fleet growth outside Austin, providing foundational scaling momentum despite low numbers.

title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla launched unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston on April 18, expanding from Austin to three Texas cities with initial geofences of about 25 square miles each. This triples Tesla's active Robotaxi hubs in Texas, aligning with H1 2026 plans for seven U.S. cities.
impact_reason: Launching unsupervised service in two major new Texas cities directly accelerates overall fleet scaling velocity by establishing additional operational hubs for rapid data collection and expansion beyond Austin.

title: TechCrunch Reports Tesla Robotaxi Launch in Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla announced unsupervised Robotaxi rollout in Dallas and Houston, now operating in three Texas cities after Austin launch last year and unsupervised start in January 2026. Tracker data shows one vehicle each in the new cities versus 46 in Austin.
impact_reason: Confirms expansion to new cities with unsupervised operations, supporting broader Texas scaling even if initial fleet addition is minimal.
